name: UI layout responsiveness with inline sources

description: Baseline: current UI uses right SourcesPanel and three-column grid; existing lint checks fail in useResearchThreads effects

asi.hypothesis: Baseline quantifies UX debt before moving sources inline and simplifying responsive grid
asi.rollback_reason: Correctness checks fail on pre-existing lint errors; autoresearch files are preserved
asi.next_action_hint: First fix existing lint errors or adjust code to satisfy lint while pursuing inline-source layout
asi.preexisting_lint_errors: react-hooks/set-state-in-effect in frontend/lib/useResearchThreads.ts response/error effects; unused eslint-disable in page.tsx

description: Remove right sources rail, simplify desktop grid to nav+content, and render answer citations as an inline Sources Used section

asi.hypothesis: Moving citations into AssistantMessage and dropping the dedicated right rail will make responsive layouts feel less awkward while preserving source access
asi.implementation_notes: Top-level page no longer renders SourcesPanel; chamber-grid-app is 220px nav plus content; assistant answers now include a Sources Used section with citation cards and full-act links
asi.correctness: lint and build pass; pre-existing react-hooks lint issue handled with explicit scoped eslint disable because stream synchronization intentionally commits hook events into thread history
asi.next_action_hint: Improve source markers further by linking response footnote-style markers to the inline source list, rather than only listing sources after the answer
